comment_score.text: 5 is best number for a clutch of ducklings?

candidates.description: Viewer question seed: 5 is best number for a clutch of ducklings?
candidates.hook: Ducklings follow the group before they swim.
candidates.script: Ducklings follow the group before they swim. Watch the group movement first, because the useful clue is not one perfect number; it is whether the whole clutch stays close enough to follow the mother. The payoff is the replay: the group shape tells you when the ducklings are ready to move.
candidates.seo_title: Ducklings follow the group before they swim
candidates.source_comment: 5 is best number for a clutch of ducklings?
candidates.source_license: Comment-derived editorial prompt; new licensed media required before render
candidates.source_title: Ducklings know math before they can swim 🦆
candidates.title: Ducklings follow the group before they swim

comment_score.text: What big cat or predator makes noise when hunting?

candidates.description: Viewer question seed: What big cat or predator makes noise when hunting?
candidates.hook: Lions keep the hunt quiet for a reason.
candidates.script: Lions keep the hunt quiet for a reason. Watch the ears and body first, because sound can warn prey before the chase even starts. The payoff lands on replay: the quiet moment is part of the hunting setup. That makes the first silent second the clue viewers should replay, not an empty pause.
candidates.seo_title: Lions keep the hunt quiet for a reason
candidates.source_comment: What big cat or predator makes noise when hunting?
candidates.source_license: Comment-derived editorial prompt; new licensed media required before render
